summary.title: AI-Powered Customer Support Platform
summary.overview: Northwind is replacing a 9-year-old contact-center platform with an AI-native customer support layer. The system must handle 18M annual contacts across retail banking, mortgage, and wealth management. Vendor must integrate with Salesforce Financial Services Cloud and the in-house core banking system.

strengths: AI workflow automation is core capability
Citibank case study is directly relevant for FFIEC reviewers
Pricing model fits per-seat + outcome bonus structure
weaknesses: SOC 2 Type II refresh dated Feb 2026 — Northwind expects ≤90 days
Only 2 financial services references on file; RFP requires 3
No Salesforce FSC integration in past deployments — need partner attestation

compliance.requirement: Vendor must provide SOC 2 Type II report dated within 90 days of submission.
compliance.note: Current report is 87 days old at submission — within window but tight; recommend addendum letter.

compliance.requirement: Provide a minimum of three (3) financial services customer references.
compliance.note: 2 of 3 references on file. Need to confirm a third FS reference willing to be cited.

compliance.requirement: Submit detailed technical implementation plan with phased milestones.
Vendor must integrate with Salesforce Financial Services Cloud.
compliance.note: No prior FSC integration documented. Recommend partner attestation from implementation partner Slalom.

compliance.requirement: Provide pricing schedule using Exhibit B template, including volume discounts.

evidence.snippet: Tiered pricing with 12% volume discount at 1M+ contacts

compliance.requirement: Maintain $5M cyber liability insurance, certificate dated within current year.

evidence.snippet: Coverage active but certificate expired Dec 2025

compliance.note: Renewal in flight with The Hartford. ETA May 4.

compliance.requirement: Describe data handling: encryption at rest and in transit, key management.

evidence.snippet: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, AWS KMS

compliance.requirement: Vendor must commit to 99.9% uptime SLA with documented incident response.
Diversity & inclusion attestation, including supplier diversity reporting.
compliance.note: Statement exists but Northwind requires a signed attestation form (Exhibit D).

compliance.requirement: Provide signed W-9 and certify no debarment or sanctions exposure.

evidence.snippet: W-9 on file in compliance binder

compliance.requirement: Describe AI model governance, including bias testing and audit logs.

evidence.snippet: Quarterly bias audit, immutable audit log via Datadog

compliance.requirement: Executive sponsor sign-off letter with named accountable executive.

items: Confirm whether the SOC 2 Type II auditor will issue a bridge letter dated within 90 days of submission.
Send updated penetration test results from Q1 2026 to attach as Exhibit C.
Validate that audit log retention meets Northwind's 7-year requirement.
Secure a third financial services reference. Options on table: Truist (warm), JPMC (cold), Ally Bank (warm).
Confirm Citibank reference is approved for written attribution in the proposal.
Decide deployment partner for Salesforce Financial Services Cloud integration. Slalom and Deloitte are options.
Commit to a 6-month full rollout vs. 9-month phased rollout.
Renewed insurance certificate from The Hartford — ETA May 4.
Provide signed executive sponsor letter naming accountable executive (likely yourself).
Approve pricing strategy: list price, 8% strategic discount, or anchor with outcome-based bonus.

draft.body: Northwind Financial Group's customers expect retail-grade digital experiences, and the contact center is the single largest determinant of NPS in retail banking. Lyra AI proposes to replace the legacy IVR-and-ticket model with an AI-native customer support layer that resolves 62% of contacts without agent involvement and accelerates the remaining 38% with in-line agent assist. Our work with [Citibank Fraud Triage] and [First Republic outbound servicing] is directly transferable to Northwind's retail, mortgage, and wealth lines.

Lyra deploys as a managed SaaS platform with secure VPC peering into Northwind's AWS environment. Our core architecture combines a retrieval layer over Northwind's policy and procedure corpus, a routing layer that classifies intent and risk, and a response layer that produces grounded answers with full source citations. Integration with Salesforce Financial Services Cloud will be delivered via [INTEGRATION PARTNER — pending Jake's decision].

Lyra maintains a SOC 2 Type II audit covering security, availability, and confidentiality. Data is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.3. AWS KMS manages keys with quarterly rotation. Audit logs are immutable and retained for [LOG RETENTION PERIOD — confirm Marcus]. AI model governance includes quarterly bias audits and red-team exercises documented in our governance handbook.

We propose a [6-MONTH OR 9-MONTH — Jake to confirm] phased rollout: discovery (weeks 1–4), retail banking pilot (weeks 5–12), mortgage and wealth expansion (weeks 13–20), and full production cutover (weeks 21–24). Each phase concludes with a measurable success gate signed by the joint steering committee.

Our proposed structure combines a per-seat platform license, a contact-volume tier, and an optional outcome-based bonus tied to deflection rate. Detailed pricing is provided in Exhibit B. We are open to negotiating an anchor commitment in exchange for [DISCOUNT POSTURE — Maya to approve].

Three financial services references are provided in Exhibit E: Citibank (fraud triage), First Republic (outbound servicing), and [THIRD REFERENCE — Priya to secure]. Each reference is current within the past 12 months and is approved for written attribution.
